meta.subject: Pre-Mathematics and Science
meta.philosophy: The Pre-Mathematics and Science syllabus is developed and designed to cater for early learners and focuses on foundational skills. Mathematics and Science are two interconnected disciplines that shape our understanding of the world around us. Section B is for 4-5 year olds. The course is designed to spark curiosity, nurture foundational skills and instil a love for learning in the young minds. The approach of combining Mathematics and Science in this syllabus is premised on understanding that children engage with a Scientific mind which seeks answers from facts and phenomena around them.
meta.competence_learning: At ECE level, assessment is an integral part of the whole teaching and learning that the developmental milestones are achieved by the learners. Assessment in Zambian Languages to be done through observation and participation. This will include the four language skills (listening, speaking, reading and writing). Teachers are not limited to these but are encouraged to assess the learners through different assessment strategies. Through assessment, teachers will be able to know each learner's ability and weaknesses and where necessary.
goals: Develop analytical thinking: identify patterns, compile data, create mental images, evaluate solutions
Develop communication skills using mathematical and scientific language in different situations
Foster creativity and innovation by exploring objects around them
Encourage collaboration through solving puzzles in groups and playing with peers
Build critical thinking: ask and answer simple questions, classify objects, manipulate objects, solve simple problems
Promote environmental sustainability: dispose waste in designated places
Develop financial literacy: identify forms of money, demonstrate ability to use money, make purchasing choices
Build problem solving: use numeracy patterns and relations to solve problems

topics.topic_title: Exploring My World - Grouping of Things
specific_outcomes: Group things according to their characteristics

Explore the surroundings to identify living things (plants: grass; animals: chicken, cow, dog, snake, insects) and non-living things (stone, toys, crayons, pencil, book) according to given criteria (quantity and size)
Group living things and non-living things according to characteristics (e.g. feeding, breathing, growth) and according to given attributes (size and colour)
Identify living things (trees, grass, animals: chicken, insects, cows, worms) and non-living things (water, stone, soil, crayons, pencil, book)
Recognise numbers by counting orally (numbers 1-20)
Count living and non-living things (1-20)
Identify written numbers using things (1-20)
Recognise the number names (1-20)
Sequence objects up to 20
Sequence number cards (1-20)
Sequence things up to 20
Recognise written number names up to 20
Write numbers up to 20 using different criteria
Match things to numbers (1-20)

topics.topic_title: Exploring My World - Things in the Surroundings
specific_outcomes: Identify coins and small notes up to K10 through story telling such as buying and selling
Count money up to K10
Form patterns using coins and small notes up to K10 (pattern game)
Care for money
Sequence money (coins and notes) according to pattern
Set an easy budget
Identify waste from the surroundings
Dispose waste items in bins or designated places
Sort out waste (garbage, liquid waste, used oil, damaged and electric toys, left over food stuff)
Participate in waste management activities such as Reduce, Reuse, Recycle
Compare lengths and weights of different objects
Measure length and weight of different things using non-standard units

topics.topic_title: Understanding My Body - The Five Senses
specific_outcomes: Care for parts of the human body associated with the five senses
Recognise the five senses: smelling, tasting, feeling, seeing, hearing through songs
Count the five senses
Make basic shapes of the sense organs using different materials
Compare shapes of the sense organs according to characteristics (size, big and small, different various shapes)
Match senses with corresponding body parts (eyes, ears, skin, nose, tongue) using games and songs
Recognise and name body parts associated with the five senses (eyes, ears, nose, tongue)
Use pictures/diagrams of the human body to identify body parts associated with the five senses and their functions
Demonstrate care for the five senses
Match the body parts with the five senses

topics.topic_title: Exploring the Sky and Seasons - Time and Seasons
specific_outcomes: Tell time by activities of the day
Tell time by using activities of the day
Tell the days of the week
Recognise the characteristics of daytime and night time
Identify visible components of the sky: sun (day) and moon (night)
Create patterns using materials such as clouds
Recognise the seasons: dry and wet in Zambia through stories, songs
Observe weather changes
Compare pictures of the environment during different seasons
Discuss why some changes happen over time
Recognise effects of climate change in the local environment

topics.topic_title: Understanding Materials - Shapes, Patterns and Light
specific_outcomes: Create simple shapes
Explore materials in the environment
Construct shapes using materials
Make lines using real objects
Create 2-dimensional diagrams
Make 3-dimensional shapes
Compare shapes of different materials according to size
Trace and draw shapes using different materials
Recognise sinking and floating objects in everyday life
Collect various materials (rock, leaf, plastic toy) and predict whether each floats, discuss the reason behind predictions
Demonstrate how shadows are formed
Explore the formation of shadows using a source of light and things
Observe shadows outdoors on a sunny day and discuss what objects need to block light to make shadows
Compare shapes of different shadows according to length and width

topics.topic_title: Exploring Mixtures - Adding and Subtracting Things
specific_outcomes: Use mathematical operations in everyday life
Explore concrete objects in the environment
Add concrete objects up to the sum of 20
Subtract concrete objects up to the difference of 20
Add different locally available materials (e.g. coloured beads, combining different snack mixtures)
Discuss how items remain separate but together
Make simple mixtures using colours (e.g. using a paint brush)
